title: Telex Watcher (external deterministic watch loops)
summary: The builder passed the Watcher viability gate after successful low-noise PR lifecycle dogfood. Production Watcher contract node #110 is ready; runtime work still waits on that contract and the shared Application Client checkpoint.

nodes.title: Vertical spike: generic external detector runner
nodes.summary: Build one provider-neutral persistent external watcher: trusted local detector commands, versioned structured results, opaque state, fixed Telex target/sender, state commit after durable send, restart recovery, and editable GitHub/Azure DevOps examples including repository-specific filtering. No agent session retains a background waiter.

nodes.title: External detector hosting is broadly useful
nodes.summary: Builder dogfoods materially different GitHub, customized GitHub, Azure DevOps, and non-PR watches. The gate passes only if agents can author detectors quickly, watches survive outside sessions, and Telex wakeup is more reliable and less disruptive than background loop tasks.

nodes.title: Production Watcher contract and application-client requirements
nodes.summary: Promote successful spike semantics into the intended design: detector envelope, watch lifecycle, script pin/follow policy, credentials and environment, failure notification, state/dedup transaction, service identity, message conventions, and the exact requirements exported to the shared Telex Application Client issue #12.

nodes.title: Production Telex Watcher runtime and CLI
nodes.summary: Implement the separately installable in-repo Watcher application under the accepted contract: supervised per-user lifecycle, durable registry and state, bounded scheduler and execution, application-client integration from #12, Telex event transaction, inspection/control CLI, diagnostics, and backend/profile support. Sketch; detail after watcher-contract and the campaign application-client checkpoint.

nodes.title: Agent-authored detector templates and skill guidance
nodes.summary: Package editable, tested detector templates and agent guidance for GitHub PRs, Azure DevOps PRs, HTTP/JSON, and command/file conditions. Templates demonstrate protocol and safe state practice without moving provider semantics into the runtime. Sketch; detail after watcher-contract.

nodes.title: Production Watcher and detector authoring are usable
nodes.summary: Builder validates the production runtime and template workflow together: create or adapt detectors rapidly, operate several watches concurrently, inspect failures and provenance, and receive predictable Telex events without session background tasks.

nodes.title: Watcher reliability, security, packaging, and scale hardening
nodes.summary: Exercise restart and upgrade, malformed/hung detectors, credential and log handling, rate limits, jitter/backoff, duplicate events, many concurrent watches, SQLite/Postgres targets, terminal lifecycle, script updates, daemon failures, and install/troubleshooting behavior. Produce validation evidence for closure. Sketch; detail after usable-watcher-gate.

nodes.title: External watch loops reliably deliver Telex attention
nodes.summary: Builder validates several real long-duration watches, including GitHub and Azure DevOps, confirms session queues remain free of background waiters, reviews hardening evidence, and accepts the Watcher/runtime/template exports and campaign integration with Operator Station.

checkpoints.title: Generic external Watcher proven valuable
checkpoints.summary: A provider-neutral detector loop works outside agent sessions and has been dogfooded across GitHub, Azure DevOps, and custom conditions.

checkpoints.title: Production Watcher and detector templates usable
checkpoints.summary: The accepted contract, application-client integration, runtime, and editable template workflow work together and have passed builder validation.

checkpoints.title: Telex Watcher operationally validated
checkpoints.summary: Long-duration external observation and Telex event delivery have survived realistic reliability, security, packaging, and scale validation.
